title: anagallis arvensis subsp. phoenicea
intro: Anagallis arvensis subsp. phoenicea, commonly known as the red pimpernel, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the primrose family. Traditionally, Anagallis arvensis subsp. phoenicea has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Anagallis arvensis subsp. phoenicea include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system. Anagallis arvensis subsp. phoenicea can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various ailments.
